title: Révision A1
description: A consolidating review that pulls together everything from the A1 course: greetings, numbers, gender and articles, être and avoir, family, colors, ordering food, directions, the past, telling time, food shopping, and the near future.

cards.notes: The all-purpose daytime greeting (Lesson 01). In the evening: **Bonsoir**.
Politeness essential. *Merci beaucoup* — thank you very much.
Numbers (Lesson 02): un, deux, **trois**, quatre, cinq. *Il est trois heures* — it's three o'clock.
Feminine noun, indefinite article **une** (Lesson 03). Food vocabulary too (Lesson 13).
Present of **être** (Lesson 04). *Je suis fatigué* — I am tired.
Family + possessives (Lesson 05). *Ma* before a feminine noun: **ma** mère, **mon** père.
Colors (Lesson 06). *Une pomme rouge* — a red apple. The adjective comes after the noun.
At the restaurant (Lesson 07). *L'addition, s'il vous plaît* — the bill, please.
Directions (Lesson 08). Opposite: *à droite* — on the right. *Tout droit* — straight ahead.
Past-time marker (Lesson 10), pairs with the passé composé. *Hier, j'ai mangé* — yesterday I ate.
Daily routine + telling time (Lesson 11). *Le matin, je bois un café* — in the morning, I drink a coffee.
Future-time marker, pairs with the futur proche. *Demain, je vais travailler* — tomorrow I'm going to work.
Polite request (Lesson 07/13). *Je voudrais un café* — I would like a coffee. Softer than *Je veux* (I want).

steps.title: What you've learned
steps.body:
# Révision A1 — you made it!

Congratulations. This is the final lesson of the French A1 course, and it's a chance to bring everything together. Look how much you can now do in French:

- **Greetings** — *Bonjour*, *Bonsoir*, *Merci*, *Au revoir* (Lesson 01)
- **Numbers** — *un, deux, trois...* (Lesson 02)
- **Gender and articles** — *le / la / un / une* (Lesson 03)
- **The present of être and avoir** — *Je suis*, *J'ai* (Lesson 04)
- **Family and possessives** — *ma mère*, *mon père* (Lesson 05)
- **Colors and clothing** — *rouge*, *bleu*, *un pull* (Lesson 06)
- **Ordering food** — *Je voudrais...*, *l'addition* (Lesson 07)
- **Directions** — *à gauche*, *à droite*, *tout droit* (Lesson 08)
- **The passé composé** — *avoir* + participle, and *être* + participle for movement verbs (Lessons 10, 12)
- **Telling time and daily routine** — *Il est trois heures*, *le matin* (Lesson 11)
- **Food shopping** — *au marché*, *une pomme* (Lesson 13)
- **The near future (futur proche)** — *je vais* + infinitive (Lesson 14)

That's a real foundation. In this lesson you'll **produce** French, not just recognise it — short sentences that mix several of these topics together, the way real conversation does.

exercise.prompt: Warm up by matching these key words from across the course with their English meanings.
Say *hello* and *thank you* in one short phrase (greeting first, then thanks).
exercise.hint: Two of the very first words you learned: *Bonjour* + *merci*.

exercise.prompt: Arrange the tiles to mean: *My name is Marie and I am twenty years old.*
exercise.hint: *Je m'appelle...* (my name is) + *et* (and) + *j'ai ... ans* (I am ... years old). French uses *avoir* for age!

exercise.prompt: Which one means *I would like the bill*?
Translate: *My mother is on the left.*
Translate: *I went to the market yesterday.* (Use the passé composé. *Aller* takes **être**, not *avoir*.)
accept: Je suis allé au marché hier.
Je suis allée au marché hier.
je suis alle au marche hier.
exercise.hint: *Je suis* + *allé* (past participle of *aller*) + *au marché* + *hier*. Movement verbs use *être*.
distractors: Je suis aller au marché hier.

exercise.prompt: Arrange the tiles to mean: *Tomorrow I am going to visit Paris.*
exercise.hint: Time marker first: *Demain* + *je vais* (futur proche) + the infinitive *visiter* + *Paris*.

exercise.prompt: Translate: *a red apple.* (Remember: the color comes after the noun.)
exercise.hint: *une* (feminine indefinite article) + *pomme* + *rouge*. The adjective follows the noun in French.

steps.title: Moving toward A2
steps.body:
## Where to go next

You've finished A1. Here's how to keep that knowledge alive and grow it toward **A2**:

1. **Use the spaced-repetition deck.** Every word you missed in these lessons comes back for review at just the right moment. Five minutes a day beats an hour once a week.
2. **Speak aloud.** Read the example sentences out loud. *Bonjour*, *Je voudrais un café*, *Demain je vais visiter Paris.* Hearing your own voice builds confidence and pronunciation faster than silent reading.
3. **Label things at home.** Stick notes on objects: *la porte* (the door), *la fenêtre* (the window), *le frigo* (the fridge). You'll absorb gender and vocabulary without trying.
4. **Build tiny sentences daily.** Mix what you know: *Hier, j'ai mangé une pomme rouge.* *Le matin, je bois un café.* Combining topics is exactly what A2 asks of you.

> Language learning is a marathon, not a sprint. You've built a real foundation — *bravo !* Keep going, little by little, and A2 will feel natural before you know it.

**Bonne continuation !** (Keep up the good work!)
